title: Microsoft Responsible AI Standard v2 Reference Guide
summary: Reference guide describing Microsoft's Responsible AI Standard and implementation expectations.
auditUse: Use as a practical control-pattern reference for responsible AI governance.

title: FactSheets for AI Services
summary: Introduces supplier declarations of conformity as factsheets for AI services.
auditUse: Use as a documentation pattern for vendor AI systems and third-party assurance.

title: Assessment List for Trustworthy Artificial Intelligence
organization: European Commission High-Level Expert Group on AI
summary: Self-assessment list translating trustworthy AI requirements into practical questions.
auditUse: Use as a question bank for qualitative AI governance and ethics reviews.

title: International AI Safety Report 2025
summary: International expert synthesis of evidence on advanced AI capabilities, risks, and safety practices.
auditUse: Use to ground frontier AI audit programs in current risk evidence, including misuse, bias, hallucination, jailbreaks, and safeguard limitations.

title: International AI Safety Report 2026
summary: Second international report reviewing scientific research on the capabilities and risks of general-purpose AI systems.
auditUse: Use as a current evidence base for advanced-AI assurance, risk taxonomies, safety evaluations, and monitoring priorities.

title: Algorithmic Impact Assessments: A Practical Framework for Public Agency Accountability
summary: Practical framework for public agencies to assess and disclose algorithmic systems before and during deployment.
auditUse: Use to design public-sector AIA procedures, disclosure requirements, stakeholder review, and external accountability mechanisms.

title: Artificial Intelligence: Agencies Have Begun Implementation but Need to Complete Key Requirements
summary: GAO review of federal agency implementation of AI requirements and remaining gaps.
auditUse: Use as a benchmark for agency AI inventory, governance, policy implementation, and federal accountability expectations.

title: Artificial Intelligence: DOD Needs Department-Wide Guidance to Inform Acquisitions
summary: GAO report on AI acquisition challenges and department-wide guidance needs at the Department of Defense.
auditUse: Use for auditing AI procurement, acquisition governance, lifecycle ownership, and vendor oversight in public-sector contexts.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Health Care: Benefits and Challenges of Machine Learning Technologies for Medical Diagnostics
summary: GAO technology assessment of machine learning for medical diagnostics, including benefits, challenges, and policy options.
auditUse: Use for healthcare AI risk reviews involving clinical validation, bias, data quality, privacy, and human oversight.

title: Understanding Algorithms
summary: Public-audit report explaining algorithmic systems and how government auditors can understand and assess them.
auditUse: Use as a public-sector audit primer for algorithm inventories, algorithm understanding, and control assessment.

title: An Audit of Algorithms: Nine Algorithms Used by the Dutch Government
summary: Audit report reviewing nine algorithms used by the Dutch government.
auditUse: Use as a concrete public-sector algorithm audit example and evidence model.

title: Oversight in the AI Era: Understanding the Audit Committee's Role
organization: Harvard Law School Forum on Corporate Governance
source: Harvard Law School Forum on Corporate Governance
summary: Governance brief on the audit committee's role in AI oversight and risk governance.
auditUse: Use to frame board and audit committee questions around AI strategy, controls, disclosure, and assurance.

title: AI Oversight: 6 Priorities for Audit Committees
summary: Practitioner guidance on AI oversight priorities for audit committees.
auditUse: Use to shape executive and board-level AI audit questions around risk, controls, reporting, and accountability.

title: Responsible AI and Internal Audit: What You Need to Know
summary: Practitioner overview of responsible AI considerations for internal audit functions.
auditUse: Use to translate responsible AI principles into internal audit planning, control review, and assurance activities.

title: Common Elements of Frontier AI Safety Policies
summary: Comparative analysis of common elements across frontier AI safety policies.
auditUse: Use to compare frontier developer safety policies, risk thresholds, escalation rules, and public commitments.

title: Towards a Common Reporting Framework for AI Incidents
summary: OECD report proposing common approaches for AI incident reporting.
auditUse: Use to design incident definitions, reporting fields, escalation pathways, and monitoring governance.

title: AI Incidents: Key Components for a Mandatory Reporting Regime
organization: Center for Security and Emerging Technology
summary: Policy report outlining key components of mandatory reporting regimes for AI incidents.
auditUse: Use when building or auditing incident reporting programs, thresholds, recipients, confidentiality, and remediation workflows.

title: HELM Safety: Towards Standardized Safety Evaluations of Language Models
source: Stanford Center for Research on Foundation Models
summary: Benchmark/report work extending the HELM evaluation approach toward standardized safety evaluations for language models.
auditUse: Use to frame safety evaluation coverage, benchmark selection, and comparable LLM safety reporting.
